filter_index.pickup_city: Pokhara Top and Top Hotel, Pokhara
filter_index.video_title: Complete Guide to the Annapurna Basecamp and Khopra Ridge | Nepal Treks | Indiahikes

snow_or_rain_notes: Snow patches may be present on the trail in Spring (April) above 12,000 feet
No snow on the trek in Autumn (October)
Spring: rain can occur if clouds cover the sun, dropping temperatures to around 15 °C
Autumn: rain or slight drizzle can occur and brings down daytime temperature by a few degrees
temperature_notes: Spring (April) nighttime: 10 °C to 5 °C, dipping to almost zero
Autumn (October) nighttime: 5 °C to -5 °C, dipping to 0 °C in October

terrain_challenges: Dense forests, open ridges, and rugged moraine sections
Rocky and uneven terrain requiring careful footing
Loose scree on Khayer Lake trail
Multiple sections prone to landslides and rockfalls
Every day includes ascents, descents and boulder sections on steep, uneven trails
altitude_or_weather_risks: Significant ascent from Tadapani to Khayer Lake via Khopra Ridge with rapid altitude gain increases chance of Acute Mountain Sickness (AMS)
At altitudes above 10,000 ft, chances of being hit by AMS are real; altitude sickness can happen to anybody including first-time and experienced trekkers
Snow accumulation at higher altitudes can increase risk of altitude sickness due to colder temperatures
Sudden rainfall can make trails muddy and slippery
Snowfall at higher altitudes (above 12,000 ft) can block paths, requiring new trails to be cut

fitness_benchmark: Walk, jog, or run 5 km in under 32 minutes
At least 3 runs from the last 1 month must meet the trek's fitness criteria
approval_requirements: Fitness approval from the Indiahikes team is required 20 days before the trek date
Only running, jogging, or walking are accepted as fitness proofs; cross-training workouts are not considered valid
prep_window: 6 to 8 weeks before the trek begins
A 6-week plan is provided for trekkers who are not currently active

experience_themes: Spring is moist; Autumn is dry and crisp
Spring has slightly warmer daytime temperatures than Autumn
Spring may have hard snow patches at higher altitudes; Autumn has no snow on the trek
Spring is when winter has just left the slopes with snow patches above 12,000 feet
Snow patches may be present on the trail in Spring (April) above 12,000 feet
No snow on the trek in Autumn (October)
primary_watchouts: Autumn: keep a jacket handy due to rain/drizzle cooling
Spring: rain under cloud cover can drop daytime temperature to ~15 °C
Dense forests, open ridges, and rugged moraine sections
Rocky and uneven terrain requiring careful footing

video_title: Complete Guide to the Annapurna Basecamp and Khopra Ridge | Nepal Treks | Indiahikes
